BASED ON NWS STORM SURVEY...INTENSE STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH WINDS OF 80 TO 100 MPH WAS FOUND FROM THE SLEEPING BEAR DUNES AREA INTO THE TOWN OF GLEN ARBOR. THOUSANDS OF TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN WITH NUMEROUS STRUCTURES ALSO DAMAGED. TIME IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR...BUT THE PERIOD OF STRONGEST WINDS OCCURRED ALONG THE LEELANAU COUNTY LAKE SHORE FROM APPROXIMATELY 408 PM UNTIL 430 PM.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS UP TO 110 MPH...A PATH LENGTH OF NEARLY 9 MILES AND A PATH WIDTH OF APROXIMATELY 75 YARDS. EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR M-33 3 MILES SOUTH OF ROSE CITY AND TRACKED EAST NORTHEAST FOR NEARLY 9 MILES BEFORE LIFTING 3 MILES SOUTHEAST OF LUPTON. TORNADO DAMAGE WAS SPORADIC THROUGHOUT THE LIFE OF THE EVENT. TYPE OF DAMAGE THAT OCCURRED INCLUDED DOWNED AND SNAPPED OFF TREES ALONG WITH DOWNED TREES ONTO HOMES AND STRUCTURES.
BUILDING DAMAGE ON THAYER ROAD REPORTED BY COUNTY DISPATCH. DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH RADAR SIGNATURE. NWS WILL CONFIRM WITH SURVEY SCHEDULED FOR WEDNESDAY.
